simulation_goal_structured: Primary objective: reduce end-to-end procurement cycle time from purchase requisition to invoice payment (target direction: minimize). Secondary objective: improve buyer and analyst utilization (target direction: maximize). Constraint: minimize delays in the quotation analysis and purchase order approval stages while maintaining throughput. Scope: end-to-end purchasing process.

kpis.name: Average Cycle Time
kpis.description: End-to-end elapsed time from purchase requisition submission to invoice payment settlement.

smart_breakdown.specific: Measures total elapsed time from the moment a buyer submits a purchase requisition until the invoice is settled.
smart_breakdown.measurable: Difference between the end timestamp of the last activity and start timestamp of the first activity per case, in hours.
smart_breakdown.achievable: Current baseline derived from SIMOD simulation; improvements expected through resource reallocation and parallelisation.
smart_breakdown.relevant: Directly addresses the primary simulation goal of reducing procurement cycle time.
smart_breakdown.time_bound: Measured per case, aggregated over the simulation run.

kpis.name: Average Waiting Time
kpis.description: Mean idle time between consecutive activities across all cases, reflecting delays in handoffs and approvals.

smart_breakdown.specific: Captures the average time cases spend waiting between the completion of one activity and the start of the next.
smart_breakdown.measurable: Sum of inter-activity gaps per case divided by number of gaps, averaged across all cases, in hours.
smart_breakdown.achievable: Reducing waiting time is feasible by improving resource availability and reducing batching delays.
smart_breakdown.relevant: Directly addresses minimizing delays in quotation analysis and PO approval stages.
smart_breakdown.time_bound: Measured per case, aggregated over the simulation run.

kpis.name: Resource Utilization
kpis.description: Average proportion of available working hours during which buyers and analysts are actively processing tasks.

smart_breakdown.specific: Measures the fraction of scheduled calendar hours that procurement resources spend on active case work.
smart_breakdown.measurable: Sum of active processing durations divided by total available calendar hours, as a ratio (0-1).
smart_breakdown.achievable: Current utilisation from SIMOD baseline; target improvement through better workload distribution.
smart_breakdown.relevant: Directly addresses the secondary goal of improving buyer and analyst utilization.
smart_breakdown.time_bound: Aggregated over the full simulation run.

kpis.name: Cost per Case
kpis.description: Average total resource cost incurred per procurement case from requisition to payment.

smart_breakdown.specific: Measures the average monetary cost of processing one procurement case through all activities.
smart_breakdown.measurable: Sum of (resource_cost_per_hour * processing_hours) per case, averaged across all cases, in EUR.
smart_breakdown.achievable: Cost reduction achievable through efficiency improvements that reduce processing time per case.
smart_breakdown.relevant: Complements cycle time reduction — ensures improvements don't come at disproportionate cost increase.
smart_breakdown.time_bound: Measured per case, aggregated over the simulation run.

kpis.name: Throughput
kpis.description: Number of procurement cases completed per day during the simulation period.

smart_breakdown.specific: Measures how many procurement cases are completed per calendar day.
smart_breakdown.measurable: Total completed cases divided by simulation time span in days.
smart_breakdown.achievable: Maintaining or improving throughput while reducing cycle time ensures no capacity regression.
smart_breakdown.relevant: Acts as a safeguard: cycle time improvements should not reduce overall throughput.
smart_breakdown.time_bound: Aggregated over the full simulation run.

reasoning: The five KPIs cover the three dimensions of the simulation goal: cycle time and waiting time for the primary minimisation objective, resource utilization for the secondary maximisation objective, cost per case as an efficiency measure, and throughput as a safeguard to ensure process capacity is maintained.
